summary.text: The dominant pattern today is the continued acceleration in MCP/Tool Calling and Coding Agent categories. DeusData/codebase-memory-mcp, a high-performance MCP server for codebase indexing, added 2,327 stars in a single day, maintaining its sustained hot trend. OpenMontage, an open-source agentic video production system, added 848 stars, also sustained hot. These two categories together account for the highest average daily deltas (2,009 and 1,578 respectively), far outpacing Local LLM/Inference (71 avg) and Other AI (354 avg). Meanwhile, Hacker News discussions show strong developer curiosity about AI brand perception (1,045 votes) and SpaceX's rumored $60B acquisition of Cursor (1,127 votes), indicating broader industry attention on AI tooling consolidation. The cross-source signal from leaked OpenAI financials and Claude Design updates further reinforces the theme of AI tooling maturity. Looking forward, the sustained high velocity in MCP and coding agents suggests these categories may continue to dominate as developers seek more integrated, agentic workflows.

projects.name: codebase-memory-mcp
projects.description: High-performance code intelligence MCP server. Indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph — average repo in milliseconds. 158 languages, sub-ms queries, 99% fewer tokens. Single static binary, zero dependencies.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A high-performance MCP server that indexes entire codebases into a persistent knowledge graph in milliseconds, supporting 158 languages with a single static binary.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents struggle to understand large codebases efficiently, often requiring many tool calls and tokens for file-by-file exploration. This tool provides instant structural queries via a knowledge graph, reducing token usage by 99% and tool calls by 2.1x.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project gained 2,327 stars in the last 24 hours, reaching 6,430 total stars with 526 forks. Its sustained hot trend (acceleration 1.0) and recent release within 30 days indicate strong ongoing interest. The combination of extreme performance (Linux kernel in 3 minutes) and zero-dependency deployment makes it timely as AI coding agents proliferate.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits (within 7 days) maintain momentum, and the arXiv paper (2603.27277) provides academic credibility.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a critical bottleneck for AI-assisted coding: enabling agents to understand large codebases instantly without excessive token costs. The plug-and-play support for 11 coding agents, 158 languages, and built-in graph visualization make it broadly appealing.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a production-ready tool that can dramatically improve the efficiency of AI coding agents. Developers should evaluate it for their workflows, especially if they work with large monorepos or multi-language projects.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend signals a shift toward persistent, graph-based code understanding for AI agents, moving away from stateless file-by-file approaches. This could become a standard component in AI-assisted development stacks.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, etc.) who need fast, token-efficient codebase understanding. Also useful for teams managing large codebases or multi-service architectures.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is written in C with zero dependencies, which may limit community contributions. The 100 open issues suggest active development but also potential bugs. As a binary that reads code and modifies agent configs, security-conscious users should audit the source.

projects.name: OpenMontage
projects.description: World's first open-source, agentic video production system. 12 pipelines, 52 tools, 500+ agent skills. Turn your AI coding assistant into a full video production studio.

llmSummary.oneLiner: OpenMontage is the first open-source, agentic video production system that turns AI coding assistants into full video studios with 12 pipelines and 52 tools.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It eliminates the need for manual video editing by letting users describe a video in plain language; the AI agent handles research, scripting, asset generation, editing, and rendering, including real motion footage from free archives.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project has 5,749 stars with a 24-hour delta of +848 stars, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity suggests broad developer interest in agentic video production workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and recently updated, and the project has active discussions and YouTube content, but no recent release or commit within 7 days.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It uniquely combines open-source video production with agentic AI, offering a free path to create real videos (not just slideshows) using stock footage, local TTS, and Remotion/HyperFrames rendering. The low-cost examples ($0.15–$1.33) and support for multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) make it accessible and viral.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: OpenMontage demonstrates a new paradigm: AI coding assistants can orchestrate complex multimedia pipelines. Developers can extend or customize the 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and 500+ agent skills to build their own video generation workflows.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward agentic media production where AI assistants handle end-to-end creative tasks. For AI developers, it opens opportunities to build specialized pipelines, integrate new video/audio providers, or optimize cost-performance trade-offs in generative media.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers, content creators, indie filmmakers, and anyone using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) who wants to automate video production without manual editing.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is AGPLv3 licensed, which may limit commercial use. It relies on third-party APIs (FAL, Pexels, etc.) for advanced features; free-tier users get limited functionality. The agentic workflow may produce inconsistent results depending on the AI assistant's capabilities.

projects.name: Unciv
projects.description: Open-source Android/Desktop remake of Civ V

llmSummary.oneLiner: An open-source, moddable remake of Civilization V for Android and desktop, built with Kotlin and LibGDX.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a free, open-source, cross-platform 4X strategy game that runs on low-end hardware and is highly moddable, filling the gap for a FOSS alternative to commercial Civ V.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 10,773 stars, a 24-hour delta of +223, and sustained commit activity (last push within 7 days), Unciv is experiencing steady, organic growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating no sudden spike but consistent attention from the open-source gaming community.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent releases and active development (roadmap includes G&K and BNW mechanics) keep the community engaged.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It's a polished, feature-rich Civ V clone that is actively maintained, cross-platform, and moddable, appealing to both nostalgic gamers and FOSS enthusiasts.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A strong example of how to build a community around a game clone by focusing on moddability, cross-platform support, and transparent roadmap.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, this project demonstrates the value of rule-based AI in game development (e.g., automation, enemy AI) and the potential for integrating LLM-based agents for more sophisticated decision-making in 4X games.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Gamers who want a free, lightweight Civ V experience; modders; open-source enthusiasts; developers interested in game AI or LibGDX.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Legal risk from Firaxis (though mechanics are not copyrightable, the project avoids using original assets or the Civilization name). Also, the scope of implementing full G&K and BNW mechanics is large and may take years.

projects.name: rlm
projects.description: General plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), supporting various sandboxes.

signals: topics 较少，分类信号较弱

llmSummary.oneLiner: A plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs) that enables LLMs to programmatically decompose and recursively process near-infinite contexts via code-execution sandboxes.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Standard LLMs struggle with long contexts and complex multi-step reasoning. RLMs replace the single completion call with a recursive, code-driven loop where the model can examine, decompose, and recursively call itself, offloading context to a REPL environment and enabling near-infinite context handling without architectural changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project has 4,999 stars and gained 200 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than explosive, but the sustained_hot trend type and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing community interest. The combination of a new arXiv paper (Dec '25), a blogpost, and integration with training frameworks (prime-rl) keeps the project in the spotlight.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and the addition of a training environment (verifiers/prime-rl) likely contributed to continued interest, but there is no sudden spike in commits or external buzz.
llmSummary.whyTrending: RLMs propose a paradigm shift in how LLMs handle long contexts and tool use, moving away from JSON tool-calling to a code-native recursive approach. The library is production-ready with multiple sandbox options (local, Docker, Modal, Prime, etc.) and integrates with popular training frameworks, making it immediately useful for researchers and builders.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: RLMs offer a drop-in replacement for standard LLM completions that can dramatically improve performance on long-context and multi-step tasks. Developers should evaluate whether their use cases (e.g., code generation, data analysis, agentic workflows) benefit from recursive decomposition and code execution.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward code-native agent architectures where LLMs interact with environments programmatically rather than via structured tool calls. For AI developers, adopting RLMs could mean better handling of complex, context-heavy tasks and a path to training custom recursive models.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI researchers exploring long-context reasoning, developers building agentic systems, and teams working on LLM-based code generation or data analysis pipelines.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (first release in 2025) and may have undiscovered edge cases. The recursive approach increases inference cost and latency. Sandbox environments require additional setup and may introduce security risks if not properly isolated.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices like phones, embedded systems, and edge devices without heavy dependencies.

llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing improvements. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for easy PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which may have attracted new users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is trending because it offers a lightweight, dependency-free solution for running neural networks on mobile and edge devices, a critical need as AI moves to the edge. Its support for multiple backends (CPU, Vulkan) and frameworks (PyTorch, ONNX) makes it versatile.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building mobile or edge AI apps, ncnn provides a production-ready inference engine with minimal overhead and broad platform support. The pnnx tool simplifies model conversion from PyTorch/ONNX, lowering the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward on-device AI inference without cloud dependencies. Developers should consider ncnn for latency-sensitive or privacy-preserving applications on Android, iOS, and embedded Linux.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile app developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and developers working with Android, iOS, or Vulkan.

projects.name: ts-pattern
projects.description: 🎨 The exhaustive Pattern Matching library for TypeScript, with smart type inference.

llmSummary.oneLiner: TS-Pattern brings exhaustive, type-safe pattern matching to TypeScript, enabling concise and readable branching on complex data structures with smart type inference.
llmSummary.problemSolved: TypeScript lacks native pattern matching, forcing developers to use verbose if/else or switch statements that are error-prone and lack exhaustiveness checking. TS-Pattern provides a compact, expressive API with full type safety and exhaustive matching.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo shows a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 3 (high), gaining 4 stars in the last 24 hours and maintaining a 7-day average of 1.5 stars/day. With 15,022 total stars and only 166 forks, the star-to-fork ratio is extremely high, indicating broad interest. The recent commit within 7 days and the sudden acceleration suggest renewed attention, possibly from a viral post or community highlight.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent commit and high acceleration may be due to a new feature or bug fix, but no release or external buzz is detected.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Pattern matching is a highly desired feature in TypeScript, and TS-Pattern is the most mature library offering it with exhaustive checking and tiny bundle size (~2kB). Its popularity is driven by the functional programming trend and the need for safer, more readable conditionals.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you work with complex state machines, reducers, or deeply nested data in TypeScript, TS-Pattern can drastically reduce boilerplate and eliminate runtime errors from unhandled cases.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a growing demand for functional programming patterns in TypeScript, especially for frontend state management. AI developers building type-safe agents or data pipelines can leverage TS-Pattern for robust branching logic.
llmSummary.targetUsers: TypeScript developers building frontend apps with complex state (React, Vue), functional programmers, and anyone tired of nested if/else or switch statements.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The library adds a runtime dependency (~2kB) and may have a learning curve for developers unfamiliar with pattern matching. Exhaustive checks only work with `.exhaustive()`, not `.otherwise()`, which could lead to incomplete coverage if misused.

hotProjects.name: rlm

signals: topics 较少，分类信号较弱

llmSummary.oneLiner: A plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs) that enables LLMs to programmatically decompose and recursively process near-infinite contexts via code-execution sandboxes.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Standard LLMs struggle with long contexts and complex multi-step reasoning. RLMs replace the single completion call with a recursive, code-driven loop where the model can examine, decompose, and recursively call itself, offloading context to a REPL environment and enabling near-infinite context handling without architectural changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project has 4,999 stars and gained 200 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than explosive, but the sustained_hot trend type and recent release (within 30 days) suggest ongoing community interest. The combination of a new arXiv paper (Dec '25), a blogpost, and integration with training frameworks (prime-rl) keeps the project in the spotlight.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and the addition of a training environment (verifiers/prime-rl) likely contributed to continued interest, but there is no sudden spike in commits or external buzz.
llmSummary.whyTrending: RLMs propose a paradigm shift in how LLMs handle long contexts and tool use, moving away from JSON tool-calling to a code-native recursive approach. The library is production-ready with multiple sandbox options (local, Docker, Modal, Prime, etc.) and integrates with popular training frameworks, making it immediately useful for researchers and builders.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: RLMs offer a drop-in replacement for standard LLM completions that can dramatically improve performance on long-context and multi-step tasks. Developers should evaluate whether their use cases (e.g., code generation, data analysis, agentic workflows) benefit from recursive decomposition and code execution.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward code-native agent architectures where LLMs interact with environments programmatically rather than via structured tool calls. For AI developers, adopting RLMs could mean better handling of complex, context-heavy tasks and a path to training custom recursive models.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI researchers exploring long-context reasoning, developers building agentic systems, and teams working on LLM-based code generation or data analysis pipelines.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (first release in 2025) and may have undiscovered edge cases. The recursive approach increases inference cost and latency. Sandbox environments require additional setup and may introduce security risks if not properly isolated.

modelDemoSignals.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
modelDemoSignals.description: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. O
modelDemoSignals.summary: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. OmniAgent executes on-demand actions to selectively distill audio-visual cues into a persistent textual memory, effectively decoupling reasoning complexity from raw video duration. To operationalize this, we introduce (1) Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning to bootstrap native active perception via best-of-N trajectory synthesis with dual-stage quality control, and (2) Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA (Turn-aware Adaptive Uncertainty Rescaled Advantage), which leverages turn-level entropy to steer credit assignment toward pivotal discovery turns. Crucially, OmniAgent exhibits positive test-time scaling, where performance improves as the number of reasoning turns increases, validating the efficacy of active perception. Empirical results across ten benchmarks (e.g., VideoMME, LVBench) demonstrate that OmniAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models. Notably, on LVBench, our 7B agent outperforms the 10$\times$ larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B (50.5% vs. 47.3%).

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
modelDemoSignals.description: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {
modelDemoSignals.summary: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {Turing-RL} uses a discriminative Turing reward with an LLM judge to score how indistinguishable a generated response is from the real user's given the user's history, and the user simulator LLM learns to produce responses indistinguishable from what the user could have said with such rewards. Across two different domains--conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion--we find that {Turing-RL} consistently outperforms baseline methods on both LLM and human evaluation metrics. Our study suggests that optimizing for indistinguishability, rather than response matching, is effective for learning user simulators.

modelDemoSignals.description: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. modelDemoSignals.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS - the Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States - a comprehensive corpus and county-harmonized access layer for U.S. municipal and county ordinance codes. The raw corpus, available for release to researchers, represents nearly all publicly available municipal and county ordinance codes. The resulting raw corpus contains codes from 9,239 cities and counties. A smaller county-harmonized LOCUS access layer provides coverage for the largest 2,309 of 3,144 U.S. counties, accounting for a majority of the population. We use OCR to handle the myriad of document formats that have kept the law from being a public resource. We release the corpus with coverage metadata to support reproducibility, downstream legal AI research, and the incremental expansion of machine-readable access to local law. We train a collection of ModernBERT-based classifiers and scorers to facilitate analyzing U.S. local law among several dimensions, such as opacity and paternalism, that have not previously been studied at this scale. LOCUS-v1 and its derivative models are available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/LocalLaws/LOCUS-v1
modelDemoSignals.author: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, Diag Davenport

modelDemoSignals.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
modelDemoSignals.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mohamed Nabail, Leo Cheng, Jingmin Wang, Nicholas Rhinehart

modelDemoSignals.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
modelDemoSignals.description: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
modelDemoSignals.author: Siyi Gu, Jialin Chen, Sophia Zhou, Arman Cohan, Rex Ying

aihotHighlights.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
aihotHighlights.summary: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0（Seeduplex）API 服务并开启邀测。该模型为原生全双工端到端语音大模型，具备精准遵循、抗干扰、动态判停三大优势。可在多人对话中安静待命，指定话题出现时主动加入；支持通过自定义工具在实时交互中完成预定日历、发送邮件等任务。抗干扰力提升，误回复率与误打断率大幅降低；判停延迟缩短约250ms，复杂场景抢话比例下降40%，用户主动打断延迟缩短约300ms。适用于汽车智能座舱、智能硬件、智能客服等场景。

aihotHighlights.title: Kimi Work 新增目标模式与插件中心，6月推出额度消耗5折福利
aihotHighlights.summary: 月之暗面旗下 Kimi Work（Beta 版）新增「目标模式」，支持设定终点后由 Agent 自主循环推进任务，最长连续运行24小时，过程中人类可随时中断调整。同时上线「插件中心」，可选装百度网盘、Canva可画、钉钉、飞书、WPS、Notion、Cloudflare 等外部应用。6月限时福利期间，Kimi 电脑客户端 Work 模式所有任务会员额度消耗减半，即从0.02%降至0.01%。

aihotHighlights.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
aihotHighlights.summary: 商务部等八部门6月18日发布关于加快"人工智能+消费"发展的实施意见。其中提到加大财政资金支持，落实数码和智能产品购新政策，鼓励地方在消费品以旧换新框架内自主制定补贴，重点支持新一代智能终端消费。增加AI手机、智能电脑、智能电视、智能家居、AI眼镜、智能网联汽车、人形机器人等产品供给，培育智能穿戴消费市场。同时加快AI在居家服务、养老服务、文化旅游、住宿餐饮、教育教学等领域的应用，建设AI商品首发平台，举办"人工智能进万家"活动。

aihotHighlights.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
aihotHighlights.summary: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。

aihotHighlights.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
aihotHighlights.summary: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

raw.titleEn: Show HN： cuTile Rust：用 Rust 编写的、安全且无数据竞争的 GPU 内核

aihotHighlights.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
aihotHighlights.summary: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

aihotHighlights.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
aihotHighlights.summary: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

aihotHighlights.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
aihotHighlights.summary: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

aihotHighlights.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

aihotHighlights.title: 库克：AI 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨，iPhone 等苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"
aihotHighlights.summary: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。
aihotHighlights.description: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布
aihotHighlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布，新增 `/config key=value` 语法允许在提示中直接设置任意配置项，新增 `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` 选项使沙盒命令支持 Apple Events，新增 `CLAUDE_CLIENT_PRESENCE_FILE` 环境变量用于抑制移动端推送通知。内置 Bun 运行时升级至 1.4，改进了长段落流式输出（逐行显示）和 API 连接中断后自动重试。子 agent 面板优化：空闲 agent 30 秒自动隐藏、列表最多 5 行。修复了提示缓存读取、Write/Edit 在网络驱动器产生 0 字节文件、启动性能回归（约 120ms）、启动阻塞（最长 15 秒）、macOS TUI 冻结、子 agent 时长显示错误、API 重试指示器残留、AWS 凭证刷新等问题。

crossSourceHighlights.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。
crossSourceHighlights.whyItMatters: 同一趋势被 2 个来源、2 条信号捕捉。

items.title: Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
items.summary: 6月17日，Claude Design 更新，支持跨项目使用统一设计系统，并与Claude Code同步工作流。用户可直接拖拽、对齐和缩放画布元素，编辑器稳定性大幅提升。设计系统可从GitHub、设计文件或原始上传导入，团队管理员可锁定标准系统防止篡改。新增桌面端侧边栏入口及独立网页端claude.ai/design。使用限制与聊天、Claude Cowork、Claude Code共享，每次任务消耗更少token，错误率下降。支持导出PDF、PPT，集成Adobe、Canva、Gamma等工具。发布首周用户超一百万。

items.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
items.description: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0（Seeduplex）API 服务并开启邀测。该模型为原生全双工端到端语音大模型，具备精准遵循、抗干扰、动态判停三大优势。可在多人对话中安静待命，指定话题出现时主动加入；支持通过自定义工具在实时交互中完成预定日历、发送邮件等任务。抗干扰力提升，误回复率与误打断率大幅降低；判停延迟缩短约250ms，复杂场景抢话比例下降40%，用户主动打断延迟缩短约300ms。适用于汽车智能座舱、智能硬件、智能客服等场景。

items.title: Kimi Work 新增目标模式与插件中心，6月推出额度消耗5折福利
items.description: 月之暗面旗下 Kimi Work（Beta 版）新增「目标模式」，支持设定终点后由 Agent 自主循环推进任务，最长连续运行24小时，过程中人类可随时中断调整。同时上线「插件中心」，可选装百度网盘、Canva可画、钉钉、飞书、WPS、Notion、Cloudflare 等外部应用。6月限时福利期间，Kimi 电脑客户端 Work 模式所有任务会员额度消耗减半，即从0.02%降至0.01%。

items.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
items.description: 商务部等八部门6月18日发布关于加快"人工智能+消费"发展的实施意见。其中提到加大财政资金支持，落实数码和智能产品购新政策，鼓励地方在消费品以旧换新框架内自主制定补贴，重点支持新一代智能终端消费。增加AI手机、智能电脑、智能电视、智能家居、AI眼镜、智能网联汽车、人形机器人等产品供给，培育智能穿戴消费市场。同时加快AI在居家服务、养老服务、文化旅游、住宿餐饮、教育教学等领域的应用，建设AI商品首发平台，举办"人工智能进万家"活动。

items.title: DeepSeek 识图模式正式上线 App 和网页端
items.description: DeepSeek 识图模式于6月18日在网页和 App 端正式上线，与快速模式、专家模式并列。开启后用户可直接上传图片让 DeepSeek 识别图像，能力超越简单文字提取。目前 App 端仍显示"图片理解功能内测中"，网页端无此提示。该模式背后的多模态模型技术细节于今年4月公开，核心框架为"Thinking with Visual Primitives（以视觉原语思考）"。

items.title: 免费开源乔木画布：AI生图+抠图，一键部署Vercel
items.description: 乔木画布推出免费开源在线图像编辑器，可一键部署Vercel为网站，功能类似简化版PS。支持Seedream和GPT-image-2生图、图片模板存储分享、一键抠图、2万图标和常见Emoji，甚至能绘制PRD。随时创建3：4/16：9/21：9等不同尺寸画布。原计划高级功能收费，庆祝端午节现全免费开源。在线体验：https：//ps.qiaomu.ai/，GitHub见评论区。

items.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
items.description: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。

items.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
items.description: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

items.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
items.description: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
items.description: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0（Seeduplex）API 服务并开启邀测。该模型为原生全双工端到端语音大模型，具备精准遵循、抗干扰、动态判停三大优势。可在多人对话中安静待命，指定话题出现时主动加入；支持通过自定义工具在实时交互中完成预定日历、发送邮件等任务。抗干扰力提升，误回复率与误打断率大幅降低；判停延迟缩短约250ms，复杂场景抢话比例下降40%，用户主动打断延迟缩短约300ms。适用于汽车智能座舱、智能硬件、智能客服等场景。

items.title: Kimi Work 新增目标模式与插件中心，6月推出额度消耗5折福利
items.description: 月之暗面旗下 Kimi Work（Beta 版）新增「目标模式」，支持设定终点后由 Agent 自主循环推进任务，最长连续运行24小时，过程中人类可随时中断调整。同时上线「插件中心」，可选装百度网盘、Canva可画、钉钉、飞书、WPS、Notion、Cloudflare 等外部应用。6月限时福利期间，Kimi 电脑客户端 Work 模式所有任务会员额度消耗减半，即从0.02%降至0.01%。

items.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
items.description: 商务部等八部门6月18日发布关于加快"人工智能+消费"发展的实施意见。其中提到加大财政资金支持，落实数码和智能产品购新政策，鼓励地方在消费品以旧换新框架内自主制定补贴，重点支持新一代智能终端消费。增加AI手机、智能电脑、智能电视、智能家居、AI眼镜、智能网联汽车、人形机器人等产品供给，培育智能穿戴消费市场。同时加快AI在居家服务、养老服务、文化旅游、住宿餐饮、教育教学等领域的应用，建设AI商品首发平台，举办"人工智能进万家"活动。

trendEntities.summary: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Agent-Reach is an open-source CLI tool that gives AI agents the ability to read and search multiple social media and content platforms (Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu) without requiring API fees.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has seen explosive star growth (33,964 stars, +7,901 in 7 days), indicating strong developer interest in MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools that extend AI agent capabilities to real-time web data. This aligns with the broader trend of AI agents needing external data access.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI agent developers, MCP tool builders, developers building autonomous agents or copilots, product managers exploring agent-based features.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enables AI agents to access real-time social and content data without per-API costs, potentially reducing integration friction for agent-based products. However, reliance on scraping may raise legal/ToS concerns.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a ready-to-use MCP server for multiple platforms, saving development time. Developers can integrate it into their agent workflows to give agents web-reading capabilities.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High star count may be driven by novelty and marketing; production reliability and maintenance are unproven. Scraping may violate platform terms of service. No evidence of commercial adoption yet.

trendEntities.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before sending them to an LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95% while preserving answer quality. It offers a library, proxy, and MCP server.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant GitHub attention (32.5k stars, +17.9k in 7 days) as developers seek cost-efficient ways to handle large context windows in LLM workflows, especially for coding agents and RAG pipelines.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered applications, especially those using coding agents, RAG systems, or handling large logs/outputs; platform engineers optimizing LLM costs.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Potential to reduce LLM API costs and latency for applications that process large volumes of text, improving scalability and user experience.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in solution to compress inputs without changing prompts or models, with multiple integration options (library, proxy, MCP).
llmSummary.riskNotes: Compression may lose nuance in some edge cases; production adoption is unproven; stars reflect attention, not proven reliability.

trendEntities.summary: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and reduces token usage by 60-90% via caching, deduplication, and compression, implemented as a single Rust binary with zero dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (2.4k in 7 days) and high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers using LLM APIs in CI/CD or development workflows, engineering managers seeking to reduce cloud costs, and tooling builders in the MCP ecosystem.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to manage LLM API costs at scale, potentially saving teams 60-90% on token spend for common dev commands.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in proxy that requires no code changes, making it easy to integrate into existing workflows and reduce token consumption immediately.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Star growth may be inflated by novelty and marketing; production adoption and long-term maintenance are unproven. Token reduction claims may vary by use case.

items.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
items.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
normalizedKeys: title:claude design 更新 跨项目保持品牌一致 与claude code协同
title:claude design now stays on brand for daily work

items.title: Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
items.summary: 6月17日，Claude Design 更新，支持跨项目使用统一设计系统，并与Claude Code同步工作流。用户可直接拖拽、对齐和缩放画布元素，编辑器稳定性大幅提升。设计系统可从GitHub、设计文件或原始上传导入，团队管理员可锁定标准系统防止篡改。新增桌面端侧边栏入口及独立网页端claude.ai/design。使用限制与聊天、Claude Cowork、Claude Code共享，每次任务消耗更少token，错误率下降。支持导出PDF、PPT，集成Adobe、Canva、Gamma等工具。发布首周用户超一百万。

trendEntities.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
normalizedKeys: title:火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3 0 api 服务 开启邀测

items.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
items.summary: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0（Seeduplex）API 服务并开启邀测。该模型为原生全双工端到端语音大模型，具备精准遵循、抗干扰、动态判停三大优势。可在多人对话中安静待命，指定话题出现时主动加入；支持通过自定义工具在实时交互中完成预定日历、发送邮件等任务。抗干扰力提升，误回复率与误打断率大幅降低；判停延迟缩短约250ms，复杂场景抢话比例下降40%，用户主动打断延迟缩短约300ms。适用于汽车智能座舱、智能硬件、智能客服等场景。

trendEntities.title: Kimi Work 新增目标模式与插件中心，6月推出额度消耗5折福利
normalizedKeys: title:kimi work 新增目标模式与插件中心 6月推出额度消耗5折福利

items.title: Kimi Work 新增目标模式与插件中心，6月推出额度消耗5折福利
items.summary: 月之暗面旗下 Kimi Work（Beta 版）新增「目标模式」，支持设定终点后由 Agent 自主循环推进任务，最长连续运行24小时，过程中人类可随时中断调整。同时上线「插件中心」，可选装百度网盘、Canva可画、钉钉、飞书、WPS、Notion、Cloudflare 等外部应用。6月限时福利期间，Kimi 电脑客户端 Work 模式所有任务会员额度消耗减半，即从0.02%降至0.01%。
items.description: 月之暗面旗下 Kimi Work（Beta 版）新增「目标模式」，支持设定终点后由 Agent 自主循环推进任务，最长连续运行24小时，过程中人类可随时中断调整。同时上线「插件中心」，可选装百度网盘、Canva可画、钉钉、飞书、WPS、Notion、Cloudflare 等外部应用。6月限时福利期间，Kimi 电脑客户端 Work 模式所有任务会员额度消耗减半，即从0.02%降至0.01%。

trendEntities.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
normalizedKeys: title:八部门 用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策 支持消费者购买 ai 相关产品

items.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
items.summary: 商务部等八部门6月18日发布关于加快"人工智能+消费"发展的实施意见。其中提到加大财政资金支持，落实数码和智能产品购新政策，鼓励地方在消费品以旧换新框架内自主制定补贴，重点支持新一代智能终端消费。增加AI手机、智能电脑、智能电视、智能家居、AI眼镜、智能网联汽车、人形机器人等产品供给，培育智能穿戴消费市场。同时加快AI在居家服务、养老服务、文化旅游、住宿餐饮、教育教学等领域的应用，建设AI商品首发平台，举办"人工智能进万家"活动。

trendEntities.title: DeepSeek 识图模式正式上线 App 和网页端
normalizedKeys: title:deepseek 识图模式正式上线 app 和网页端

items.title: DeepSeek 识图模式正式上线 App 和网页端
items.summary: DeepSeek 识图模式于6月18日在网页和 App 端正式上线，与快速模式、专家模式并列。开启后用户可直接上传图片让 DeepSeek 识别图像，能力超越简单文字提取。目前 App 端仍显示"图片理解功能内测中"，网页端无此提示。该模式背后的多模态模型技术细节于今年4月公开，核心框架为"Thinking with Visual Primitives（以视觉原语思考）"。

items.title: 免费开源乔木画布：AI生图+抠图，一键部署Vercel
items.summary: 乔木画布推出免费开源在线图像编辑器，可一键部署Vercel为网站，功能类似简化版PS。支持Seedream和GPT-image-2生图、图片模板存储分享、一键抠图、2万图标和常见Emoji，甚至能绘制PRD。随时创建3：4/16：9/21：9等不同尺寸画布。原计划高级功能收费，庆祝端午节现全免费开源。在线体验：https：//ps.qiaomu.ai/，GitHub见评论区。

trendEntities.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
normalizedKeys: title:moss tts local transformer v1 5 在 sglang omni 上 原生流式 48 khz 语音服务

items.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
items.summary: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。
items.description: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。

trendEntities.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
normalizedKeys: title:cutile rust 安全无数据竞争的 gpu 内核系统

items.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
items.summary: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

trendEntities.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
normalizedKeys: title:首个统一科学大模型 logos 正式开源

items.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
items.summary: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

trendEntities.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
normalizedKeys: title:上交所发布指引 ai大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准

items.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
items.summary: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

trendEntities.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
normalizedKeys: title:苹果 xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 ai 智能体 支持自然语言修 bug 构建 app

items.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
items.summary: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

trendEntities.title: 库克：AI 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨，iPhone 等苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"
normalizedKeys: title:库克 ai 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨 iphone 等苹果产品涨价已 不可避免

items.title: 库克：AI 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨，iPhone 等苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"
items.summary: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。
items.description: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。

trendEntities.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布

items.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布
items.summary: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布，新增 `/config key=value` 语法允许在提示中直接设置任意配置项，新增 `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` 选项使沙盒命令支持 Apple Events，新增 `CLAUDE_CLIENT_PRESENCE_FILE` 环境变量用于抑制移动端推送通知。内置 Bun 运行时升级至 1.4，改进了长段落流式输出（逐行显示）和 API 连接中断后自动重试。子 agent 面板优化：空闲 agent 30 秒自动隐藏、列表最多 5 行。修复了提示缓存读取、Write/Edit 在网络驱动器产生 0 字节文件、启动性能回归（约 120ms）、启动阻塞（最长 15 秒）、macOS TUI 冻结、子 agent 时长显示错误、API 重试指示器残留、AWS 凭证刷新等问题。

trendEntities.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
normalizedKeys: title:google 分享 a2ui 与 mcp apps 三种集成架构模式

items.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
items.summary: Google 分享了三种集成 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 的架构模式，旨在结合两者优势。A2UI 采用声明式框架，通过 JSON payload 定义 UI，由宿主原生渲染，确保一致性与安全性，但受限于预定义组件库。MCP Apps 在 iframe 中使用标准 Web 技术提供自定义界面，但存在设计碎片化、性能与安全挑战。三种模式包括：通过 MCP 服务器提供 A2UI，利用 MCP Resources 或 Tool 调用传递 JSON，实现"一次编写，原生渲染"的跨平台能力；以及静态与动态交付方案。Google 正考虑扩展 MCP 以原生支持 A2UI。

raw.titleEn: A2UI + MCP Apps： Combining the best of declarative and custom agentic UIs

sourceItems.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
sourceItems.summary: Google 分享了三种集成 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 的架构模式，旨在结合两者优势。A2UI 采用声明式框架，通过 JSON payload 定义 UI，由宿主原生渲染，确保一致性与安全性，但受限于预定义组件库。MCP Apps 在 iframe 中使用标准 Web 技术提供自定义界面，但存在设计碎片化、性能与安全挑战。三种模式包括：通过 MCP 服务器提供 A2UI，利用 MCP Resources 或 Tool 调用传递 JSON，实现"一次编写，原生渲染"的跨平台能力；以及静态与动态交付方案。Google 正考虑扩展 MCP 以原生支持 A2UI。

trendEntities.title: 借助 Workload Identity Federation 安全访问 Claude Platform
normalizedKeys: title:借助 workload identity federation 安全访问 claude platform

items.title: 借助 Workload Identity Federation 安全访问 Claude Platform
items.summary: Workload Identity Federation （WIF） 已在 Claude Platform 上全面可用。WIF 兼容任何 OIDC 身份提供者，覆盖所有 Claude API 端点（包括第一方 SDK 和 Claude Code）。WIF 用短生命期凭证替代静态 API 密钥，并引入服务账户，每个工作负载拥有独立身份、角色和审计日志。Claude Console 提供引导设置流程，支持 Admin API 进行组织管理。API 密钥可并行使用以便逐步迁移。

trendEntities.title: Matt Pocock 开源 skills v1：将技能描述 Token 成本降低 63%
normalizedKeys: title:matt pocock 开源 skills v1 将技能描述 token 成本降低 63

items.title: Matt Pocock 开源 skills v1：将技能描述 Token 成本降低 63%
items.summary: Matt Pocock（Total TypeScript 作者）开源了 skills v1，将技能描述的 Token 成本降低 63%。该工具包将技能分为模型可调用和用户可调用，新增 /codebase-design、/domain-modeling、/grilling 三项技能；重写 /writing-great-skills；将 /diagnose 更新为 /diagnosing-bugs 并改为模型可调用；新增 /ask-matt 路由技能，帮助 AI 自动判断时机触发合适工程流程。主推文评价其将 prompt 从咒语拆解为纪律性流程。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic与DeepMind CEO呼吁G7组建AI联盟排除中国
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic与deepmind ceo呼吁g7组建ai联盟排除中国

items.title: Anthropic与DeepMind CEO呼吁G7组建AI联盟排除中国
items.summary: Dario Amodei（Anthropic）与Demis Hassabis（Google DeepMind）在G7闭门会议上呼吁组建美国主导的联盟，为人工智能制定全球规则和标准。Amodei指出，该联盟应以前沿模型和硬件（包括芯片及其他关键组件）的访问权限为手段，将中国排除在外。这一主张被评论为高技术新冷战的开端，竞争方将从根本上被剥夺参与权。

trendEntities.title: 用SGLang-JAX在TPU上优化Ling-2.6-1T：一个Pallas核将MoE数据移动隐藏在计算中
normalizedKeys: title:用sglang jax在tpu上优化ling 2 6 1t 一个pallas核将moe数据移动隐藏在计算中

items.title: 用SGLang-JAX在TPU上优化Ling-2.6-1T：一个Pallas核将MoE数据移动隐藏在计算中
items.summary: SGLang-JAX现已支持inclusionAI的Ling-2.6-1T（1T稀疏MoE，63B激活参数，256路由专家，top-8路由加共享专家）在TPU v7x上高效推理。团队开发了Fused MoE V2--一个融合scatter、专家FFN和gather的Pallas核，通过将MoE数据移动隐藏在计算中，使MoE预填充延迟从5.16ms降至2.42ms（降幅53%），解码核延迟从0.249ms降至0.211ms（降幅约15%）。仅替换MoE核即提升预填充吞吐量24.8%，解码吞吐量18.5%-35.3%。在SGLang解码基准测试中，16块TPU v7x芯片输出吞吐量达16块H200 GPU的1.29倍（mc=128）至1.77倍（mc=512）。完整上线还包含混合KV/循环内存池、GLA线性注意力和单控制器数据并行支持。

trendEntities.title: Vercel 发布开源 AI 智能体框架 Eve：每个智能体就是一个文件目录
normalizedKeys: title:vercel 发布开源 ai 智能体框架 eve 每个智能体就是一个文件目录

items.title: Vercel 发布开源 AI 智能体框架 Eve：每个智能体就是一个文件目录
items.summary: Vercel 发布开源 AI 智能体框架 Eve（npm 包，Apache-2.0 许可）。Eve 采用文件系统优先设计：每个智能体对应一个磁盘目录，目录结构直接映射模型、指令、工具、技能、连接、子智能体等能力，无需额外注册代码。内置六大生产级能力：持久执行（每步检查点，崩溃后可恢复）、沙箱计算、人机审批、安全连接（支持 MCP 和 OpenAPI）、多通道（Slack、Discord、Teams 等）以及追踪与评估（OpenTelemetry）。Vercel 内部运行了上百个智能体，包括数据分析工具 d0（月处理超3万查询）、自动销售代理 Lead Agent（年费约5000美元、回报32倍）和支持智能体 Vertex（自主解决92%工单）。

trendEntities.title: NVIDIA GEAR实验室发布ENPIRE：8个Codex智能体自主控制机器人完成物理实验
normalizedKeys: title:nvidia gear实验室发布enpire 8个codex智能体自主控制机器人完成物理实验

items.title: NVIDIA GEAR实验室发布ENPIRE：8个Codex智能体自主控制机器人完成物理实验
items.summary: NVIDIA GEAR实验室推出ENPIRE系统，首次实现物理世界自主研究。系统让8个Codex智能体控制8台机器人，配备GPU和token预算。安全方面采用硬运动极限切断和扭矩受限夹爪两层硬件保障，支持通宵无人运行。奖励函数通过视觉分类器离线固定并冻结，防止智能体作弊。实时监测机器人利用率（MRU）、token利用率（MTU）和GPU利用率，以Tokens-to-Success和Time-to-Success评估效率。ENPIRE自主完成扎带、整理细针、安装GPU等高精度任务，发现8机器人并行探索显著更快。系统将开源。

sourceItems.summary: NVIDIA GEAR实验室推出ENPIRE系统，首次实现物理世界自主研究。系统让8个Codex智能体控制8台机器人，配备GPU和token预算。安全方面采用硬运动极限切断和扭矩受限夹爪两层硬件保障，支持通宵无人运行。奖励函数通过视觉分类器离线固定并冻结，防止智能体作弊。实时监测机器人利用率（MRU）、token利用率（MTU）和GPU利用率，以Tokens-to-Success和Time-to-Success评估效率。ENPIRE自主完成扎带、整理细针、安装GPU等高精度任务，发现8机器人并行探索显著更快。系统将开源。

trendEntities.title: Google发布99美元Gemini智能音箱

items.title: Google发布99美元Gemini智能音箱
items.summary: Google推出首款专为Gemini打造的智能音箱Google Home Speaker，售价99.99美元。支持自然语言请求和多步指令，可在说话中途纠正，并具备连续对话功能。内置10种新声音。高级AI功能需订阅Google Home Premium（月费10美元或年费100美元），包括Gemini Live自由对话、Nest摄像头活动摘要等。即日起预售，本月发货。

trendEntities.title: 谷歌发布Agentic Resource Discovery（ARD）开放规范
normalizedKeys: title:谷歌发布agentic resource discovery ard 开放规范

items.title: 谷歌发布Agentic Resource Discovery（ARD）开放规范
items.summary: Agentic Resource Discovery（ARD）是一项开放规范，用于在Web上发布、发现和验证AI工具、技能与智能体。它基于两个原语：组织在其自有域名下托管catalog描述可用能力，registry作为搜索引擎索引catalog并响应发现请求。ARD支持加密验证，使客户端与端点连接前确认发布者身份，然后直接通过原生协议调用能力。Google Cloud的Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform通过Agent Registry提供企业级支持，包括URN命名、出站策略、工具固定和基于Agent Identity的信任验证。该规范现已发布，开发者可通过托管`ai-catalog.json`文件使其服务可发现。

trendEntities.title: Omnigent开源：AI智能体团队元框架
normalizedKeys: title:omnigent开源 ai智能体团队元框架

items.title: Omnigent开源：AI智能体团队元框架
items.summary: 编程的未来不是单一智能体，而是一个完整的AI团队。 Omnigent让你在一个实时会话中运行一个智能体团队：Claude Code、Codex、Cursor、Pi，以及你自己的智能体。 它是一个面向AI智能体的元框架，基于我们内部的Databricks开发工具构建，现已开源给所有人。 由传奇人物@matei_zaharia和Databricks AI团队打造。没错，Matei仍然编写大量代码，包括Omnigent和我们产品的前端代码。

trendEntities.title: MolmoMotion：语言引导的3D运动预测模型
normalizedKeys: title:molmomotion 语言引导的3d运动预测模型

items.title: MolmoMotion：语言引导的3D运动预测模型
items.summary: MolmoMotion基于Molmo 2骨干网络，输入视频帧、物体上的3D点标记及文字动作指令（如"移动并旋转桌上放水果的木碗"），预测未来数秒内这些点的3D轨迹。提供两个变体：自回归的MolmoMotion-AR逐步预测坐标，流匹配的MolmoMotion-FM通过连续空间变换处理多可能性运动。同时发布MolmoMotion-1M数据集（含116万视频的3D点轨迹及动作描述）和PointMotionBench基准测试（2700个人工验证视频片段）。模型权重、数据集和基准测试均已开源。

sourceItems.title: MolmoMotion：语言引导的3D运动预测模型
sourceItems.summary: MolmoMotion基于Molmo 2骨干网络，输入视频帧、物体上的3D点标记及文字动作指令（如"移动并旋转桌上放水果的木碗"），预测未来数秒内这些点的3D轨迹。提供两个变体：自回归的MolmoMotion-AR逐步预测坐标，流匹配的MolmoMotion-FM通过连续空间变换处理多可能性运动。同时发布MolmoMotion-1M数据集（含116万视频的3D点轨迹及动作描述）和PointMotionBench基准测试（2700个人工验证视频片段）。模型权重、数据集和基准测试均已开源。

trendEntities.title: Google 医学推理 AI 系统 AMIE 新研究：从诊断迈向长期疾病管理
normalizedKeys: title:google 医学推理 ai 系统 amie 新研究 从诊断迈向长期疾病管理

items.title: Google 医学推理 AI 系统 AMIE 新研究：从诊断迈向长期疾病管理
items.summary: 今日发表在《自然》杂志上的研究展示了 Google 的医学推理 AI 系统 AMIE（Articulate Medical Intelligence Explorer）从单次诊断对话演进到长期疾病管理的能力。AMIE 利用 Gemini 模型的长上下文能力，整合共情对话智能体和深度思考管理推理智能体，可交叉引用数百页临床指南。在盲测中，AMIE 与 21 名初级保健医生相比，在整体管理推理上匹配临床医生，在计划精确性和指南一致性上得分显著更高。

Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
normalizedKeys: title:native active perception as reasoning for omni modal understanding

items.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
items.description: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. O
items.summary: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. OmniAgent executes on-demand actions to selectively distill audio-visual cues into a persistent textual memory, effectively decoupling reasoning complexity from raw video duration. To operationalize this, we introduce (1) Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning to bootstrap native active perception via best-of-N trajectory synthesis with dual-stage quality control, and (2) Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA (Turn-aware Adaptive Uncertainty Rescaled Advantage), which leverages turn-level entropy to steer credit assignment toward pivotal discovery turns. Crucially, OmniAgent exhibits positive test-time scaling, where performance improves as the number of reasoning turns increases, validating the efficacy of active perception. Empirical results across ten benchmarks (e.g., VideoMME, LVBench) demonstrate that OmniAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models. Notably, on LVBench, our 7B agent outperforms the 10$\times$ larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B (50.5% vs. 47.3%).
items.author: Zhenghao Xing, Ruiyang Xu, Yuxuan Wang, Jinzheng He, Ziyang Ma, Qize Yang, Yunfei Chu, Jin Xu, Junyang Lin, Chi-Wing Fu, Pheng-Ann Heng
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark, agentic

trendEntities.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
normalizedKeys: title:learning user simulators with turing rewards

items.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
items.description: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {
items.summary: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {Turing-RL} uses a discriminative Turing reward with an LLM judge to score how indistinguishable a generated response is from the real user's given the user's history, and the user simulator LLM learns to produce responses indistinguishable from what the user could have said with such rewards. Across two different domains--conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion--we find that {Turing-RL} consistently outperforms baseline methods on both LLM and human evaluation metrics. Our study suggests that optimizing for indistinguishability, rather than response matching, is effective for learning user simulators.
items.author: Yingshan Susan Wang, Cedegao E. Zhang, Linlu Qiu, Zexue He, Pengyuan Li, Alex Pentland, Roger P. Levy, Yoon Kim
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
normalizedKeys: title:freeing the law with locus a local ordinance corpus for the united states

items.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
items.description: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS -
items.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS - the Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States - a comprehensive corpus and county-harmonized access layer for U.S. municipal and county ordinance codes. The raw corpus, available for release to researchers, represents nearly all publicly available municipal and county ordinance codes. The resulting raw corpus contains codes from 9,239 cities and counties. A smaller county-harmonized LOCUS access layer provides coverage for the largest 2,309 of 3,144 U.S. counties, accounting for a majority of the population. We use OCR to handle the myriad of document formats that have kept the law from being a public resource. We release the corpus with coverage metadata to support reproducibility, downstream legal AI research, and the incremental expansion of machine-readable access to local law. We train a collection of ModernBERT-based classifiers and scorers to facilitate analyzing U.S. local law among several dimensions, such as opacity and paternalism, that have not previously been studied at this scale. LOCUS-v1 and its derivative models are available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/LocalLaws/LOCUS-v1
items.author: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, Diag Davenport
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag

trendEntities.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
normalizedKeys: title:ubp2 uncertainty balanced preference planning for efficient preference based reinforcement learning

items.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
items.description: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
items.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.

We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
sourceItems.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.

items.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
items.description: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a s
items.summary: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
items.author: Siyi Gu, Jialin Chen, Sophia Zhou, Arman Cohan, Rex Ying
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark

trendEntities.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
normalizedKeys: title:reference driven multi speaker audio scene generation from in the wild priors

items.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
items.description: Existing multi-speaker dialogue systems bind speakers to utterances through structured supervision: per-turn tags, multi-stream transcriptions, or learnable speaker embeddings. These systems operate within speech-only pipelines that produce clean vocal sequences without the ambient texture of real conversations. We take a different approach. Our method, ScenA, conditions a text-to-audio flow-matching foundation model, pretrained on large-scale in-the-wild data, directly on multiple reference voi
items.summary: Existing multi-speaker dialogue systems bind speakers to utterances through structured supervision: per-turn tags, multi-stream transcriptions, or learnable speaker embeddings. These systems operate within speech-only pipelines that produce clean vocal sequences without the ambient texture of real conversations. We take a different approach. Our method, ScenA, conditions a text-to-audio flow-matching foundation model, pretrained on large-scale in-the-wild data, directly on multiple reference voices and a free-form natural language prompt that describes an entire multi-speaker audio scene. Leveraging such a foundational model allows us to inherit its capacity for natural, non-studio audio: background noise, room acoustics, overlapping dialogue, and spontaneous paralinguistic events, while adding multi-speaker control without any per-turn structure. Concretely, reference latents are concatenated into the model's token sequence and distinguished by lightweight identity-aware positional encodings. However, we identify a critical obstacle to this approach: the \textit{Reference Shortcut}. During training under standard noise schedules, the model can identify the matching reference by acoustic similarity to the noisy target, bypassing the text prompt entirely. We address this with a high-noise-biased timestep distribution that forces the model to rely on the text prompt for speaker assignment. We evaluate ScenA on the CoVoMix2-Dialogue benchmark, showing that it outperforms existing multi-speaker systems on speaker-binding metrics while generating rich conversational audio with overlapping speech, emotional vocalizations, and ambient sound. Our results demonstrate the advantage of using a general-purpose audio model conditioned on a free-form scene description, rather than passing structured dialog scripts through a speech-only pipeline.
items.author: Michael Finkelson, Daniel Segal, Eitan Richardson, Shahar Armon, Nani Goldring, Poriya Panet, Nir Zabari, Benjamin Brazowski, Or Patashnik, Yoav HaCohen

trendEntities.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents

items.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
items.description: Production data integration is bottlenecked by repeated, lossy handoffs between data owners, engineers, and analysts who must collaboratively discover, structure, and query enterprise data. We present Data Intelligence Agents (DIA), a system of three agents (Data Interpreter, Schema Creator, and Query Generator) that compresses this workflow by treating autonomous coding agents (ACAs) as a first-class abstraction: rather than emitting text, the agents generate, execute, validate, and repair conc
items.summary: Production data integration is bottlenecked by repeated, lossy handoffs between data owners, engineers, and analysts who must collaboratively discover, structure, and query enterprise data. We present Data Intelligence Agents (DIA), a system of three agents (Data Interpreter, Schema Creator, and Query Generator) that compresses this workflow by treating autonomous coding agents (ACAs) as a first-class abstraction: rather than emitting text, the agents generate, execute, validate, and repair concrete artifacts, draw on a shared memory for experience reuse, and surface each for review by domain experts. DIA is deployed in production for enterprise customers. We study the Query Generator in depth and evaluate it in fully autonomous mode across seven SQL benchmarks spanning four task categories and four dialects. It matches or surpasses the best published results on all seven, demonstrating that an architecture grounded in execution, built on ACAs and a shared memory, generalizes across the data intelligence workload with adaptation confined to natural-language instructions.
items.author: Anoushka Vyas, Aarushi Dhanuka, Sina Khoshfetrat Pakazad, Henrik Ohlsson

trendEntities.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis

items.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
items.description: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language m
items.summary: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language model with a summary of these matrices, and instruct it to generate a set of Python programs that can reproduce the associated attention patterns given only text from the input sentence. Finally, we re-rank programs according to how well our final set of programs predict behavior on held-out inputs. We demonstrate that a set of fewer than 1,000 such generated programs can reproduce the attention patterns of heads in GPT-2, TinyLlama-1.1B, and Llama-3B, achieving an average Intersection-over-Union similarity above 75% on TinyStories. Moreover, the best-fit programs can replace neural attention heads without substantially affecting model behavior: replacing 25% of attention heads with programmatic surrogates across the three models incurs only a 16% average perplexity increase, while maintaining performance on a variety of downstream question answering benchmarks. This work contributes a scalable pipeline for reverse-engineering attention heads in transformer models using human-readable, executable code, advancing a path toward symbolic transparency in neural models.
items.author: Amiri Hayes, Belinda Li, Jacob Andreas

items.title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation
items.description: Enhancing the formal math reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) has become a key focus in both mathematical and computer science communities in recent years. While significant progress has been made in using state-of-the-art Auto-Regressive (AR) LLMs for formal theorem proving, these models suffer from inherent limitations. Their next-token prediction generation methods may yield suboptimal performance due to the challenges of long-range coherence and the compounding of errors o
items.summary: Enhancing the formal math reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) has become a key focus in both mathematical and computer science communities in recent years. While significant progress has been made in using state-of-the-art Auto-Regressive (AR) LLMs for formal theorem proving, these models suffer from inherent limitations. Their next-token prediction generation methods may yield suboptimal performance due to the challenges of long-range coherence and the compounding of errors over long sequences. Recent advancements in diffusion LLMs (dLLMs), which generate text through iterative denoising of a multi-token block, offer a promising alternative. However, the application of dLLMs to formal mathematics, where maintaining long-range coherence is critical, remains largely understudied. To address the challenges above, we propose **Diffusion-Proof**, to the best of our knowledge, the first framework to train and apply dLLMs for formal theorem proving. Our frameworks contain training and inference methods for two models. The first one is *dLLM-Prover-7B*, which performs whole-proof writing with long-range coherent tactic usage. The second one is *dLLM-Corrector-7B*, which is a novel large block diffusion-based correction model. It leverages the in-filling capabilities of dLLMs to perform local proof correction using bi-directional information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that **Diffusion-Proof** relatively significantly outperforms the AR LLM baseline trained under the same dataset. **Diffusion-Proof** achieves an absolute improvement of **1.61%** on ProofNet-Test and **6.14%** on MiniF2F-Test benchmarks compare to the baseline. Notably, **Diffusion-Proof** successfully resolves one IMO problem that more advanced thinking model DeepSeek-Prover-V2-7B could not solve, showcasing the unique advantage of dLLMs in formal theorem proving.
items.author: Ruida Wang, Rui Pan, Pengcheng Wang, Shizhe Diao, Tong Zhang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

items.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
items.description: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems (MAS) have demonstrated great potential in solving tasks with execution complexity, by distributing subtasks across cooperative agents. However, this divide-and-conquer paradigm falls short on decision-making tasks that are also prevalent in the real world. items.summary: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems (MAS) have demonstrated great potential in solving tasks with execution complexity, by distributing subtasks across cooperative agents. However, this divide-and-conquer paradigm falls short on decision-making tasks that are also prevalent in the real world. These tasks require simultaneous reasoning from the stances of all involved stakeholders whose decisions are mutually dependent and thus cannot be solved in isolation. We characterize this challenge as stance entanglement, a form of decision complexity distinct from execution complexity. To address it, we propose Multi-Agent Fictitious Play (MAFP), a novel MAS paradigm that represents stakeholder stances as agents and formulates decision-making as an equilibrium-seeking process. Built on the game-theoretic principle of fictitious play, MAFP iteratively updates each agent's decision by best responding to the empirical mixture of other agents' past decisions. This enables agents to expose and address one another's weaknesses, progressively improving decision quality and robustness. We evaluate MAFP on challenging decision-making tasks that test the capability of deciding strategies for competitive scenarios prior to acting. MAFP outperforms both single-round and multi-round baselines on two complementary metrics, tournament strength and robustness, demonstrating its effectiveness in addressing stance entanglement.
items.author: Leyang Shen, Yang Zhang, Xiaoyan Zhao, Chun Kai Ling, Tat-Seng Chua
